summary: Conjecture 1.2 from arXiv:2108.02685 remains open in full generality. Fox, Luo, and Pham (2022) proved the asymptotic version for d-regular graphs when d = o(n/(log n)^{12}) via probabilistic methods. Ma and Xie (2024) obtained the first n-independent bound for d-regular graphs (within 2d^2 of the conjectured value) and proved the conjecture in strong form for d=3 regular graphs using deterministic local-adjustment techniques. Luzar, Przybylo, and Sotak (2024) independently resolved the cubic case exactly, showing deviation from n/4 is at most 1/2 (up to three exceptions). The exact bound for general graphs with minimum degree delta is still open.

since_posted.title: On random irregular subgraphs
since_posted.authors: Jacob Fox, Sammy Luo, Huy Tuan Pham
since_posted.claim: Proves the asymptotic version of the conjecture for d-regular graphs when d = o(n/(log n)^{12}): with high probability the irregular random subgraph satisfies m(H,k) = (1+o(1))n/(d+1) for all 0 <= k <= d.

since_posted.title: Finding irregular subgraphs via local adjustments
since_posted.claim: Gives the first n-independent bound for d-regular graphs (vertex degree counts within 2d^2 of n/(d+1)) and proves the conjecture in strong form for cubic (d=3) regular graphs via deterministic local-adjustment methods.

since_posted.title: Degree-balanced decompositions of cubic graphs
since_posted.authors: Borut Luzar, Jakub Przybylo, Roman Sotak
since_posted.claim: Resolves the Alon-Wei conjecture for cubic graphs, showing that every cubic graph on n vertices has a spanning subgraph where the number of vertices of each degree deviates from n/4 by at most 1/2, up to three exceptions.

internal_refs_verified.note: All four internal-ref entries are duplicates pointing to the same paper. Abstract confirms: Fox, Luo, Pham 2022, 'On random irregular subgraphs', proves asymptotic result for d = o(n/(log n)^{12}). Contribution descriptions in the corpus are accurate except the labelling of the asymptotic result as 'Conjecture 1.2' — in the source paper Conjecture 1.2 is the exact-bound generalisation to minimum degree delta, not its asymptotic relaxation.

search_queries: irregular subgraphs spanning subgraph minimum degree n/(delta+1) Alon Wei conjecture 2108.02685
irregular subgraph conjecture spanning subgraph minimum degree n/(d+1) arXiv 2022 2023 2024
notes: The conjecture is closely related to Conjecture 1.1 (the regular-graph version). Most follow-up work addresses the regular case; the minimum-degree generalisation of Conjecture 1.2 has seen partial progress only through the special case d=3 and asymptotic results. No paper found that resolves the full conjecture for arbitrary minimum degree delta.
